user.name: Jeffrey Towson 陶迅
user.description: I help increase online growth and strengthen digital AI moats. Digital & AI mgmt consultant. Get my free email and Tech Strategy podcast.

fullText:
TENCENT LAUNCHES WORKBUDDY AS ITS FLAGSHIP AGENT PRODUCT

Tencent has been moving fast in AI and especially agents this month. And their flagship agent product is WorkBuddy.

Here’s what makes it different (so far) 

1. It Integrates with WeChat. Giving you Mobile Control.

The platform connects to your desktop with WeChat. 

So, you can control an office PCs in person or remotely from a mobile device.

Overall, it has both cloud and local operations for task management.

2. WorkBuddy interfaces with both the OpenClaw and Hermes Agent ecosystems. 

Tencent has been coordinating with OpenClaw, recently launching QClaw (internationally)

This unifies the two major open-source agent frameworks (and their skill sets) into a single enterprise desktop tool.

3. Integration with Tencent SkillHub Ecosystem

The agent relies on Tencent’s new SkillHub marketplace, which contains over 22,000 specialized skills (lot of that is OpenClaw right now).

The short video introduction is pretty good.

#tencent #workbuddy #agents

Our team at AI Frontiers Lab at Microsoft Research had been cooking hard on this for quite a while.

Aion 1.0 is 14B model that can run locally with reasoning + tool calling capabilities. You can choose whatever agentic harness you like or make your own. Calls to the model never leaves your device and no one charges you for any tokens you use 🥳.
